threat_actor.name: Codefinger
threat_actor.motivation: financial — ransomware extortion via S3 data encryption

credential_chain.source: publicly exposed or compromised long-term IAM access key pair
credential_chain.expiration: manual rotation required; no automatic expiry

file_paths: ransom note deposited per affected S3 directory
crypto_wallets: Bitcoin address included in ransom note (value not disclosed)

attack_creates_resources: ransom note objects in each affected S3 prefix

cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Re-encrypting real S3 data with attacker-held SSE-C key causes irrecoverable data loss
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Upload synthetic test objects to isolated S3 bucket; apply SSE-C with locally generated AES-256 key; verify HMAC-only CloudTrail log entry; then delete test objects

cannot_safely_emulate.reason: S3 lifecycle deletion policies cause permanent object deletion after 7 days on production data
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Set lifecycle rule on isolated test bucket with 1-day expiry on synthetic objects; verify policy API logs; immediately remove rule after verification
